results.title: IRP1 [4Fe-4S] Cluster Occupancy as Feeding-Entrained Iron-Redox Chronostat
strengths_retained: Best-grounded hypothesis (groundedness 8)
Directly addresses unmeasured variable in Nadimpalli 2024
IRP2 KO separation test is elegant experimental design
Dual output (IRE binding + aconitase) provides mechanistic distinction
Honest magnitude assessment (2-3 fold, 15-25% contribution)
key_concerns: IRP1 may contribute too little (15-25%) to be functionally measurable above noise
IRP1 cluster half-life ~3h is parametric; no direct measurement found
IRP1-C437S mutant has chronic effects confounding circadian analysis

results.title: Frataxin-Gated Fe-S Assembly Oscillation via Mitochondrial LIP in FTMT-Negative Tissues
critical_flaws: Mitochondrial LIP amplification (20-30% > cytoplasmic 10-15%) quantitatively unjustified
Mitoferrin rate-limiting step unknown — could completely decouple serum iron from mito LIP
Multiple unverified intermediate steps in signal chain
strengths_retained: FTMT tissue specificity argument is creative and well-grounded
Leverages Lill 2025 stoichiometric constraint effectively
FA carrier translational angle is innovative
FTMT rescue experiment is excellent control
key_concerns: Quantitative chain serum iron → mito LIP → assembly rate has too many unverified steps
FTMT absence may reflect low mitochondrial iron demand, not high throughput
Serum iron in mice is 20-30% (Am J Hematol 2024), not 30-50% as cited

results.title: CISD2 [2Fe-2S] as Redox-Gated ER-Mitochondrial Calcium Timer
critical_flaws: CISD2 redox midpoint is +24 mV (Biomedicines 2021), NOT -10 mV as claimed from Zuris 2011 mitoNEET data
At cytoplasmic redox potential (-280 to -320 mV), CISD2 cluster is >99.99% reduced under ALL conditions
NAD+/NADH 30% oscillation cannot drive CISD2 cluster redox cycling — thermodynamically impossible
Entire Nernst calculation and 15% mito Ca2+ prediction collapse with correct midpoint
key_concerns: Core mechanism (NAD+/NADH → CISD2 cluster redox cycling) is thermodynamically implausible
Misattribution of mitoNEET (CISD1) data to CISD2 — these are different proteins
results.kill_reason: Wrong CISD2 redox midpoint (+24 mV actual, not -10 mV claimed) renders the core NAD+/NADH → cluster redox cycling mechanism thermodynamically implausible. Comparable to Cycle 1 H6 kill (GSH/GSSG no diurnal rhythm): the transduction mechanism does not operate as described.

results.title: Cytoplasmic Prx1/2 H2O2 Oscillation as Non-Transcriptional Timer for Cytoplasmic Fe-S Proteins
critical_flaws: ~2-3 orders of magnitude H2O2 gap: predicted pulse 10-100 nM vs IRP1 activation threshold ~1-10 µM (Pantopoulos 1998)
IRP1 H2O2 → [3Fe-4S] does NOT directly activate IRE binding in vitro (Beinert 1999); requires cellular machinery
Prx2 circadian mechanism in nucleated cells differs from RBCs (Srx repair, no hemoglobin driver)
strengths_retained: Creative connection between two ancient conserved oscillatory systems
BMAL1 KO test is strongest discriminating test for non-transcriptional mechanisms
Genuine novelty (zero publications)
Would be transformative if true
key_concerns: Quantitative H2O2 gap is the most serious concern — 100-1000× below threshold
Prx2 hyperoxidation in nucleated cells may not produce cytoplasmic H2O2 pulses like in RBCs
Multiple scavengers (catalase, GPx) compete with Prx2 H2O2 signal

results.title: NADPH→FDXR→FDX2 Electron Supply Chain as Circadian Fe-S Assembly Gatekeeper
strengths_retained: Best quantitative analysis in the set
Honest self-correction of FDXR saturation problem
Leverages cutting-edge Lill 2025 stoichiometric constraint
Partial FDX2 knockdown test directly tests in vivo sensitivity
Therapeutic implications for Friedreich's ataxia
key_concerns: FDX2 cluster half-life (~4h) is estimated, not measured
Stoichiometric sensitivity may be buffered in vivo
No published circadian data for any component (FDX2, FDXR, mito NADPH)
FDXR Km for NADPH is parametric

results.title: CIA Pathway as LIP/ROS-Responsive Circadian Gate for Cytoplasmic Fe-S Proteome
strengths_retained: Built on experimentally verified CIAO3 biology (Maio 2022)
Broad impact — 20+ cytoplasmic Fe-S proteins coordinately regulated
Concordant dual inputs (LIP + ROS) strengthen the mechanism
Excellent test design — especially mass spec pulldown
Clinical relevance via circadian DNA repair (XPD)
key_concerns: Acute perturbation → circadian extrapolation gap
Cytoplasmic LIP oscillation (~10-15%) may be below CIAO3 response threshold
Long-lived Fe-S proteins (half-life >> 24h) would not track 24h CIA oscillation
CIAO3 dose-response at physiological iron/ROS ranges unknown

results.title: Conserved Fe-S Requirement in Clock Neurons — Drosophila to Mammalian SCN
strengths_retained: Built on solid published data (5 genes, convergent phenotype in Drosophila)
14-year translational gap is striking
Clear falsifiable mammalian prediction
dCRY alternative is WEAKER than feared (dCRY is FAD, not Fe-S) — strengthens hypothesis
Good test hierarchy (cell line → ex vivo → in vivo)
key_concerns: NFS1flox/flox mouse availability uncertain
General neurodegeneration vs specific circadian disruption challenge
Mechanism may be generic (energetic failure) rather than revealing specific Fe-S ↔ clock interaction
No proposed identity for specific Fe-S protein in clock mechanism

meta_critique: Leniency check PASSED: found ≥2 factual errors (CISD2 redox midpoint, IRP1 H2O2/IRE binding oversimplification). Harshness check PASSED: no kills on magnitude alone without calculation. Vocabulary re-description check PASSED: all survivors make genuinely new predictions. Calibration check PASSED: 1/7 killed (14.3%) + 2/7 weakened (28.6%) = 42.9% impaired, consistent with stronger cycle 2 hypotheses.
critic_questions: H2.1: What is the minimum detectable IRP1 contribution to IRE regulation? Is 15-25% above native PAGE assay detection limit?
H2.2: What is the dose-response of mitochondrial LIP on frataxin activity? Is 10-15% cytoplasmic LIP oscillation sufficient after mitoferrin rate-limiting?
H2.4: Can the 2-3 order-of-magnitude H2O2 gap (10-100 nM predicted vs 1-10 µM threshold, Pantopoulos 1998) be narrowed with local concentration or slow kinetics evidence?
H2.5: Any published circadian/diurnal data for FDX2, FDXR protein, or mitochondrial NADPH? Even CircaDB transcriptomic data would help.
H2.6: What evidence exists that circadian-scale LIP oscillation (~10-15%) is above CIAO3 interaction modulation threshold? Any graded dose-response data?
